name: Harness Phase 2 — MCP Server for Claude Code native tool_use
objective:
Build a local MCP (Model Context Protocol) server so Claude Code can call RelayHQ operations as native tools (tool_use API) instead of running shell commands. This removes the friction of `bun run ./cli/relayhq.ts ...` invocations and lets Claude call relayhq_claim_task, relayhq_bootstrap, etc. directly. The MCP server is a thin HTTP→tool bridge: it delegates 100% to the existing API endpoints.

Design constraints (from critique of harness-plan):
- MCP is for Claude Code specifically. OpenCode and Antigravity use HTTP or CLI — do not try to unify all runtimes in this server.
- Zero new business logic here. Every tool is a fetch() wrapper over an existing API endpoint.
- Server runs locally on a Unix socket or a fixed port (e.g. 4311).
- After this workstream, CLAUDE.md must explain how to enable MCP.
exit_criteria: mcp/server.ts runs as a standalone Bun process exposing MCP tool protocol.
8 tools registered: relayhq_get_context, relayhq_list_tasks, relayhq_get_bootstrap, relayhq_claim_task, relayhq_heartbeat, relayhq_update_task, relayhq_request_approval, relayhq_create_tasks.
.mcp.json (or mcp.json) at repo root points to mcp/server.ts.
CLAUDE.md updated with MCP setup instructions.
Each tool delegates to an existing HTTP endpoint — no duplicated logic.
